title: Podman, QEMU, Apple Silicon (ARM) for x86_64 machines -- $HOME mount works seldomly (but sometimes)
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

Mounting host directories from an Apple Silicon OSX host into the QEMU podman machine works some, but not often.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine init`

2. edit the config to deal with apple silicon and x86 podman machine

3. `podman machine start`

**Describe the results you received:**

I received a podman machine running without the specified mount of `/Users/bob`.

```
Mounting volume... /Users/bob:/Users/bob
DEBU[0123] Executing: ssh [-i /Users/bob/.ssh/podman-machine-default -p 54099 core@localhost -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o LogLevel=ERROR -o SetEnv=LC_ALL= -q -- sudo chattr -i / ; sudo mkdir -p /Users/bob ; sudo chattr +i / ;]
Error: exit status 255
```

When booting with `trace` log-level, it appears that the `ssh` commands occur earlier in wall-clock time than I see the keys installed on the console via ignition.

```
[core@localhost ~]$ ls -l /Users/bob/
total 0
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected `/Users/bob` to be mounted on the QEMU podman machine.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

After the first `machine init` it'll work for a bit. The mount may then disappear while the machine is running. Subsequent `machine start` fail continuously with exit code 255 around the SSH chattr bits, I think. I see no attempts to `mount` but even trace logging seems relatively... light?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
bmcwhirt-mac:doctrine bob$ podman version
WARN[0000] ssh host key mismatch for host localhost:54099, got key SHA256:Jx5oadUn645hVvdj6d3pOnj5v6Wo0s5kNTT6+cKibK4 of type ecdsa-sha2-nistp256
Error: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: knownhosts: key mismatch
```

After restarting the machine

```
bmcwhirt-mac:doctrine bob$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Tue Oct 18 13:55:57 2022
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.1
API Version:  4.2.1
Go Version:   go1.18.5
Built:        Wed Sep  7 15:58:19 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


**Output of `podman info`:**

```
<<hangs>>
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**


```
bmcwhirt-mac:qemu bob$ brew info podman
==> podman: stable 4.3.0 (bottled), HEAD
Tool for managing OCI containers and pods
https://podman.io/
/opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/4.3.0 (185 files, 47.6MB) *
  Poured from bottle on 2022-11-01 at 09:28:42
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/podman.rb
License: Apache-2.0 and GPL-3.0-or-later
```
```
bmcwhirt-mac:spdx bob$ brew info qemu
==> qemu: stable 7.1.0 (bottled), HEAD
Emulator for x86 and PowerPC
https://www.qemu.org/
/opt/homebrew/Cellar/qemu/7.1.0 (163 files, 501.8MB) *
  Poured from bottle on 2022-09-26 at 15:05:25
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/qemu.rb
License: GPL-2.0-only
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Apple Silicon.

```
bmcwhirt-mac:qemu bob$ cat podman-machine-default.json
{
```

comments.body: if the machine means nothing to you, does a `machine rm` followed by `machine init --now` resolve things?
I've rm'd and init'd a few times but never with --now. I'll try in the morrow.

`machine init --now` will init and start arm64 machine on Apple Silicon. Where the issue is reported for amd64 on Apple Silicon, which obviously has very different performance. I would suggest updating the first message to contain (x86_64):
> Mounting host directories from an Apple Silicon OSX host into the QEMU **x86_64** podman machine works some, but not often.

And may be also list it in **Additional environment details**
Does this work with qemu-user-static instead of qemu-system-x86_64? I'm not sure if qemu-system-x86_64 would work properly on M1

I'll check. For what it's worth, I believe Rosetta on M1 is what allows x86 stuff to Just Work.

And I could get podman working with qemu-x86 on occasion, and while manually doing the mounts. Something in podman's mounts is what seemed to go awry.
Yeah, It's known that podman doesn't really like Rosetta much, but worth giving it a try atm.

You can get more log info with SSH verbose logging for during the create/mount step.

You can try my binary or modify `pkg/machine/qemu/machine.go` yourself: https://github.com/joeashcraft/podman/releases/tag/v4.3.0-debug

---

I think maybe the SSH command runs before SSH server is fully operational on the VM

```
$ ./bin/darwin/podman --log-level debug machine start intel
INFO[0000] ./bin/darwin/podman filtering at log level debug
Starting machine "intel"
[/opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/4.3.0/libexec/podman/gvproxy -listen-qemu unix:///var/folders/8m/ltl1806n6wg96ymzrs9sx4dm0000gn/T/podman/qmp_intel.sock -pid-file /var/folders/8m/ltl1806n6wg96ymzrs9sx4dm0000gn/T/podman/intel_proxy.pid -ssh-port 58967 -forward-sock /Users/jashcraf/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/intel/podman.sock -forward-dest /run/user/501/podman/podman.sock -forward-user core -forward-identity /Users/jashcraf/.ssh/intel --debug]
DEBU[0000] qemu cmd: [/opt/homebrew/bin/qemu-system-x86_64 -m 2048 -smp 4 -fw_cfg name=opt/com.coreos/config,file=/Users/jashcraf/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/intel.ign -qmp unix:/var/folders/8m/ltl1806n6wg96ymzrs9sx4dm0000gn/T/podman/qmp_intel.sock,server=on,wait=off -netdev socket,id=vlan,fd=3 -device virtio-net-pci,netdev=vlan,mac=5a:94:ef:e4:0c:ee -device virtio-serial -chardev socket,path=/var/folders/8m/ltl1806n6wg96ymzrs9sx4dm0000gn/T/podman/intel_ready.sock,server=on,wait=off,id=aintel_ready -device virtserialport,chardev=aintel_ready,name=org.fedoraproject.port.0 -pidfile /var/folders/8m/ltl1806n6wg96ymzrs9sx4dm0000gn/T/podman/intel_vm.pid -virtfs local,path=/Users/jashcraf,mount_tag=vol0,security_model=mapped-xattr -drive if=virtio,file=/Users/jashcraf/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/fedora-coreos-36.20221001.3.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz]
Waiting for VM ...
Mounting volume... /Users/jashcraf:/home/jashcraf
DEBU[0082] Executing: ssh [-i /Users/jashcraf/.ssh/intel -p 58967 core@localhost -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o LogLevel=ERROR -o SetEnv=LC_ALL= -v -- sudo mkdir -p /home/jashcraf]
OpenSSH_8.6p1, LibreSSL 3.3.6
...
debug1: Connecting to localhost port 58967.
debug1: Connection established.
debug1: identity file /Users/jashcraf/.ssh/intel type 3
...
debug1: Local version string SSH-2.0-OpenSSH_8.6
kex_exchange_identification: read: Connection reset by peer
Connection reset by 127.0.0.1 port 58967
Error: exit status 255
```

A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

The same problem, but on an Intel Mac. 
After some time after creating the machine the directories stop mount.
I am facing the same issue on an Intel Mac. First run somehow worked but now it fails with the mount issue
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@ashley-cui Do you see this as still a problem?
I think the mount process is unstable sometimes, as the ssh call fails. I think this issue with rosetta/machine is a little different though.
@rhatdan I don't see this issue on my MacBook M2 with current Podman.
